message:
fix(core): implement real kill_on_drop for asupersync-runtime process::Command

The kill_on_drop flag was previously a no-op stub for API compatibility.
When a Command future was dropped (e.g., on timeout), the child process
continued running indefinitely in the background, leaking resources
and potentially producing stale side effects.

Implement a cancellation mechanism using:
- KillOnDropGuard wrapping an Arc<AtomicBool> cancel flag that is set
  on Drop when kill_on_drop is enabled
- run_output_command() spawning the child with piped stdout/stderr and
  null stdin, then polling try_wait() every 10ms in a loop that also
  checks the cancel flag
- On cancellation: kill the child, wait for it, join the pipe reader
  threads, and return io::ErrorKind::Interrupted
- On normal completion: disarm the guard so the cancel flag is not set
- Pipe reading moved to separate threads (stdout_handle, stderr_handle)
  to prevent blocking the poll loop

Tests:
- process_command_kill_on_drop_stops_timed_out_child: verifies a
  sleep+write script is killed before its marker file is created
- process_command_output_matches_stdlib_stdin_null_behavior: verifies
  stdin is /dev/null (matching std::process::Command::output behavior)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
